Build an advanced Web-Based Virtual Laser Physics Laboratory for first-year engineering students (Applied Physics – Photonics).

The platform must function as a complete experiential learning system, not just a visual simulation.

Upgrade the existing stage-based Laser Action Lab to include structured laboratory format and measurable experiments.

For each stage, include the following sections:

Aim
Theory (clear and concise)
Procedure (step-by-step instructions)
Interactive Simulation
Observation Table
Graph (if applicable)
Result
Conclusion

Enhance the following stages:

Stage 1 – Fundamentals
Add explanation of laser characteristics: monochromaticity, coherence, directionality, high intensity. Include a simple visual comparison between normal light and laser light.

Stage 2 – Atomic Energy Levels
Allow comparison of 2-level, 3-level, and 4-level systems. Add a comparison table showing inversion possibility, pump power required, and efficiency.

Stage 3 – Einstein Processes
Display qualitative explanation of A21, B12, B21. Add measurable output showing process rates vs radiation density. Include a graph.

Stage 4 – Population Inversion
